description:
You are helping a developer clean-up some project metadata files that live in
/home/user/project.  
Two raw, tab-separated data files already exist:

1. /home/user/project/component_a.tsv  
   (3 tab-delimited columns: component name, version, maintainer)

2. /home/user/project/component_b.tsv  
   (2 tab-delimited columns: component name, status)

Your job is to produce a single tab-delimited file that the continuous-integration
system can consume and to leave a very small process log so the developer can
quickly see that everything ran as expected.

WHAT YOU MUST CREATE
====================
A. /home/user/project/combined_components.tsv  
   • Must contain EXACTLY three columns in the following order:
     1) component name  
     2) version  
     3) status  
   • A single header line must appear as the very first line containing the
     literal labels (lower-case, no extra spaces)  
       component<TAB>version<TAB>status  
   • After the header, list every component that appears in component_a.tsv,
     in the same order it appears there.  
   • Use tab (ASCII 0x09) as the ONLY field separator.  
   • Use Unix line endings and finish the file with a trailing newline.  
   • There must be exactly 4 data lines, one per component.

   Example of the required final format (tabs shown as ␉ for clarity):
   component␉version␉status
   comp_login␉v1.2␉stable
   comp_payments␉v2.0␉deprecated
   comp_notifications␉v1.4␉beta
   comp_search␉v1.8␉stable

B. /home/user/project/process.log  
   • Exactly two lines.  
   • First line:  SUCCESS: columns extracted  
   • Second line: SUCCESS: combined_components.tsv created  
   • Each line must be terminated with a Unix newline.  
   • No additional text, timestamps, or blank lines.

CONSTRAINTS
-----------
* Accomplish the task only with standard POSIX shell utilities;
  in particular, demonstrate the use of `cut` to slice columns
  out of the two source files and `paste` (or similar column-joining
  approach) to glue the needed columns together.
* The whole solution should reasonably fit in about 5-10 individual
  shell commands (not counting comments or line continuations).
* Do not modify the original component_a.tsv or component_b.tsv files.

VERIFICATION
------------
Automated tests will confirm all of the following:

1. /home/user/project/combined_components.tsv exists.
2. The file has exactly 5 lines (1 header + 4 data lines).
3. Each line contains exactly 3 tab-separated fields.
4. The file contents match the example above byte-for-byte, including the
   trailing newline.
5. /home/user/project/process.log exists and its two lines are
   exactly as specified (case-sensitive, no additional whitespace).

If every check passes, the grader will consider the task solved.
truth:
Pre-existing input files and their contents (already present when the agent
starts, using a tab (0x09) between columns and a trailing newline at the end
of each line):

File: /home/user/project/component_a.tsv
--------------------------------------------------
comp_login	v1.2	alice
comp_payments	v2.0	bob
comp_notifications	v1.4	carol
comp_search	v1.8	dave
--------------------------------------------------

File: /home/user/project/component_b.tsv
--------------------------------------------------
comp_login	stable
comp_payments	deprecated
comp_notifications	beta
comp_search	stable
--------------------------------------------------

Expected result file that the agent must create:

File: /home/user/project/combined_components.tsv
--------------------------------------------------
component	version	status
comp_login	v1.2	stable
comp_payments	v2.0	deprecated
comp_notifications	v1.4	beta
comp_search	v1.8	stable
--------------------------------------------------
(There is a single trailing newline after the last line.)

Expected log file that the agent must create:

File: /home/user/project/process.log
--------------------------------------------------
SUCCESS: columns extracted
SUCCESS: combined_components.tsv created
--------------------------------------------------
(Exactly two lines, each terminated with a newline, nothing else.)

The grader will check the two new files for these exact byte-for-byte
contents.
